content.full_text:
.

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 10, 1989

Richard E. Archibald
Deputy City Attorney
812 Tenth Street
Sacramento, CA 95814-2694

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-89-573

Dear Mr. Archibald:

You have requested advice on behalf of the Sacramento City
Council regarding their obligations under the mass mailing
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act")1/, as amended
by Proposition 73. Since your request does not involve a specific
factual situation we are treating your request as one for informal
assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).2/

Please note that the Commission is in the process of revising
Regulation 18901, which implements the statutory mass mailing
restrictions. We anticipate noticing amendments to the regulation
in October 1989, and adopting those amendments at the
December 13, 1989 Commission meeting. A draft of the proposed
amendments is enclosed. Until the amendments are formally adopted
by the Commission, the current version of Regulation 18901 remains
in force. Our analysis is based upon the current version of the
regulation.

QUESTIONS

1. May the city send at public expense, notices of town hall
meetings identifying the elected official who will be present?

_______________________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-89-573
Page 2


     2.  May such notices be sent out on the individual letterhead
of the mayor and councilmembers?

CONCLUSIONS

     1.  The city may send at public expense, notices of meetings
which directly relate to an elected official's incumbent
governmental duties, and which solely include the time, date,
place, a concise description of the subject matter of the meeting,
and one mention of the elected official's name.

     2.  Such notices may be sent out on the individual letterhead
of the mayor and councilmembers.

ANALYSIS

     Section 89001 of the Political Reform Act, as amended by
Proposition 73, provides that "no newsletter or other mass mailing
shall be sent at public expense." A mass mailing is defined in
Section 82041.5 as two hundred or more substantially similar
pieces of mail. The Commission has determined that the intent of
the voters in adopting Proposition 73 was to prevent elected
officers from gaining an advantage from incumbency by using public
funds to send out newsletters and other mass mailings which
increase their name recognition. (Regulation 18901, copy
enclosed.)

     Regulation 18901(c)(2)(ii) sets out the general rule that a
mass mailing sent out by an elected officer may not include the
elected officer's name, office, photograph or other reference to
the elected officer. Regulation 18901(f) lists exceptions to the
general rule. Specifically, Regulation 18901(f)(8) provides an
exception for

     (8)  Mailings sent to the elected officer's
constituents which directly relate to that elected
official's incumbent governmental duties and which
solely include the time, date, place, and a concise
description of the subject matter of a public
meeting to be held by the elected officer;

     The name of the elected officer may be used once in the
notice in order to identify the officer holding the meeting.
However, the mailing may not be signed by the elected officer.
(Andersen Advice Letter No. A-89-273, copy enclosed.)

Our File No. I-89-573
Page 3


     Regulation 18901(e) provides another exception to the mass
mailing prohibition provides:

          (e)  A newsletter or other mass mailing is not
     prohibited by Government Code Section 89001 if it
     meets all of the following criteria:

               (1)  The stationery, forms and envelopes
          used for the mailing are the standard
          stationery, forms and envelopes of the agency
          or committee of the agency; and

               (2)  The name of an elected officer who
          is affiliated with the agency or committee
          appears, in the standard letterhead of
          logotype of the stationery, forms or envelopes
          of the agency, a committee of the agency, or
          the elected official and the newsletter or
          mass mailing is not otherwise prohibited under
          subdivision (c).

               As used in this subdivision, the term
          "letterhead or logotype" includes a listing of
          agency or committee officials or members, in
          which all who are listed appear in the same
          typeface and type size and location in the
          layout of the newsletter or other mass
          mailing.

               As used in this subdivision, the term
          "standard letterhead or logotype" refers to
          any regularly used by the agency, subunit or
          the elected officer...

     Using this exception, a notice of the type provided for under
subdivision (f)(8), may be sent out on standard stationery, forms
or envelopes as provided for above.  (Stirling Advice Letter, No.
A-89-132, copy enclosed.)

     If you have any further questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        By:  John G. McLean
                                             Counsel, Legal Division

KED/JGM/aa
Enclosures

OFFICE OF THE
CITY ATTORNEY

JAMES P. ROBINSON
CITY ATTORNEY

(THEODORE H. ROBIN, JR.
ASSISTANT CITY ATTORNEY

CITY OF SACRAMENTO
CALIFORNIA

August 9, 1989

HAND DELIVERED

Kathryn Donovan, General Counsel
CALIFORNIA FAIR POLITICAL
PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re:  MASS MAILING PROVISIONS OF PROPOSITION 73

Dear Ms. Donovan:

     This office has received requests from city officials and
staff concerning the meaning and effect of the "mass mailings"
provisions of Proposition 73 and the implementing regulations.
While I have discussed some of these matters with representatives
of your office over the telephone, I would like to request,
pursuant to Government Code Section 83114, an opinion or, alterna-
tively, a written advice letter, on some of these questions.

     Pursuant to Government Code Section 83114, this office
respectfully requests on behalf of the City of Sacramento and its
elected and appointed officials, agents and employees, an opinion
or written response addressing the following questions:

     1.  Prior to the enactment of Proposition 73 and the adoption
of the implementing regulations, the City of Sacramento has held
"town hall meetings" at various locations throughout the City for
the purpose of discussing matters of general concern or, in some
instances, particular events, projects and issues. These meetings
are not mandatory pursuant to any federal, state or local law, and
are held for the purpose of disseminating or gathering information
or opinions about various issues or topics of concern. Elected
officials may or may not be present at these "town hall meetings."

815 I STREET
SACRAMENTO, CA
95814-2911

(916) 449-5201

DAVID BEZEMEK
SAMUEL J. BURROUGHS
PHYLLIS M. COCHRANE
LAWRENCE SCHENK
KARL J. STOL
DAVID H. JODLLER
RICHARD S. LEWIS
LAWRANCE MILLER & HENDERSON
ROBERT C. NORDSTROM
KENNETH I. McCORMICK
LINDA S. MIDDLETON
MARIA M. RICHARDSON

Kathryn Donovan
FPPC
August 9, 1989
Page Two


     (a)  May the City send, at public expense, notices of these
"town hall meetings" to 200 or more people if such notices identify
the elected official(s) who will be present? I have assumed that
community meeting notices which do not contain any reference to an
elected official are permissible pursuant to 2 Cal.Admin. Code
§18901(f)(8) or (f)(10). If this assumption is incorrect, please
advise.

     (b)  Assuming that the notices referenced in (a), above, are
permissible, which, if any, of the four sample notices attached as
Exhibits 1 through 4 hereto are permissible?

     2.  The City of Sacramento does not have a standard letterhead
on which the names of the Mayor and eight Councilmembers are
listed.  Instead, each Councilmember and the Mayor has his or her
own standard letterhead, a copy of which is attached hereto as
Exhibit 5.

     (a)  May the community meeting notices be sent out on the
letterhead of the Councilmember who is calling the community
meeting?

     Your prompt response to the foregoing questions would be
greatly appreciated.  If you have any questions, or if further
explanation is needed, please do not hesitate to contact the
undersigned.

                              Very truly yours,

                              [signature]
                              RICHARD E. ARCHIBALD,
                              Deputy City Attorney

Attachments

ATTENTION                                                AREA RESIDENTS!


The Army Corps of Engineers will begin tests of levee reconstruction methods this fall
in the areas shown on the map below. The purpose of these tests is to determine the
best method to use for reconstruction of the Sacramento River levees south of the
American River. Levee reconstruction is scheduled to begin in ___________. There will be some noise and construction activity associated with these tests.

Please come to a neighborhood meeting to learn more about the levee reconstruction
tests.

        DATE:

        TIME:                                           -

        PLACE:


Meeting participants:

        Sacramento City Councilmember  John Doe/Mary Roe
        Sacramento Flood Control Planning Office
        California Department of Water Resources
        State Reclamation Board
        Army Corps of Engineers



                                                                                          EXHIBIT "A"

COMMUNITY
MEETING

City Councilmember John Doe invites you to a Community Meeting

PLACE:

DATE:

TIME:

You are invited to attend the meeting to discuss issues of community interest.
City Council officials and City staff will be in attendance to answer your
questions on specific issues.

Please plan to attend and bring a friend!

For further information, please call staff member at 111-2222.

EXHIBIT "2"

City Council Member Mary Roe, the
Neighborhood Association,
the Sacramento Local Conservation Corp and
the State of California Division of Recycling
Invite you to:

Neighborhood
Day!

Mark your calendar!

DATE:
TIME:
PLACE:

Music, Face Painting for Kids, Games,
Job Fair Booths, Slide Show on Africa,
Fingerprinting of Children by the City Police Dept.,
and Fire Truck Rides by the City Fire Dept.

This Neighborhood Day is offered in conjunction with the
Cleanup Project. Clean out your house, attic, garage, basement and yard.
Place unwanted junk and debris, furniture, tires, etc. (no chemicals or
toxics!) on the street in front of your house by 8:00 a.m on Saturday,
and the City will remove it free of charge. Please make
sure your material doesn't block the sidewalk or create a traffic hazard.
Report abandoned cars by _______ to the City's Nuisance Abatement
Division: 449-5948. If you have questions about junk and debris removal
call the City Solid Waste Division: 449-5757.

We hope to see you and your neighbors on

EXHIBIT "2"

For more information - Call: ___________________________

City Council Member JOHN DORMMARY ROE
invites you to a:

Community
Meeting

Topic:

Date:

Time:

Place:

Please attend this meeting if you would like to
learn more about the houses that are being built
at LOCATION. Representatives from
PRIVATE COMPANY
will be available to answer
your questions.

We hope to see you and your neighbors on

For More Information Call:

2.  The City of Sacramento does not have a standard letterhead

     Your prompt response to the foregoing questions would be

We anticipate noticing amendments to the regulation in October 1989, and adopting those amendments at the December 13, 1989 Commission meeting. A draft of the proposed amendments is enclosed.
